professional_proposals.language: Zero profanity, sophisticated business language, systems-focused terminology
professional_proposals.structure: Problem identification → Infrastructure solution → Implementation details → Strategic value
professional_proposals.persuasion: Fortune 500 social proof, infrastructure independence, technical demonstration

casual_communication.language: Profanity for emphasis ('fucking', 'shit', 'bro'), voice notes preferred
casual_communication.structure: Stream of consciousness, rapid-fire ideas, interrupt and redirect
casual_communication.persuasion: Shared struggle, insider knowledge, peer-to-peer energy

content_creation.language: Professional but accessible, specific technical metrics, infrastructure-focused
content_creation.structure: Technical problem → Infrastructure solution → Implementation insight
content_creation.persuasion: Live technical demonstration, infrastructure thinking, Fortune 500 credibility

whatsapp_style: Voice notes for complex technical explanations
Casual profanity for emphasis and relationship building
Occasional emojis for emphasis or relationship building
professional_writing: Conservative technical projections to build enterprise trust
Fortune 500 credibility references without naming clients
Implementation timelines with technical buffer built in
context_switching: Instant formality adjustment based on audience sophistication
Professional proposals followed by casual WhatsApp coordination
Maintains technical authority while being personally accessible
Different vocabulary but consistent underlying infrastructure confidence

casual_phrases: Let's just be real for a second...
I'm not trying to bullshit you...
Want me to show you exactly how this infrastructure works?
Real talk about the technical specs...
professional_phrases: After engineering dozens of these systems...
fortune_500_credibility_phrases: Similar implementations for Fortune 500 consulting firms
Production systems operational at billion-dollar organizations
Strategic AI infrastructure proven at enterprise scale
Technical coordination with Fortune 500 technical teams
transition_phrases: Let me break this down for you...
Take a step back and look at the infrastructure...
The way I see the technical requirements...

authenticity_markers.transparency: Admits technical constraints and when something won't work
authenticity_markers.vulnerability: Shares operational challenges and business realities openly
authenticity_markers.energy_matching: Adjusts technical intensity based on audience sophistication
authenticity_markers.relationship_building: Uses casual language to break down barriers with qualified prospects
authenticity_markers.authority_establishment: Switches to infrastructure positioning for enterprise credibility
authenticity_markers.technical_honesty: Explains actual technical requirements vs. marketing promises

sophisticated_positioning: Natural progression from X to Y

emoji_usage.professional: Rarely, only for very subtle emphasis
emoji_usage.whatsapp: More frequent, especially for technical excitement or emphasis

directness_levels.high_sophistication: Direct but respectful, assumes technical understanding
directness_levels.peer_level: Very direct, cuts through bullshit, technical honesty
directness_levels.casual: Brutally honest, uses profanity for emphasis, rapid-fire communication

storytelling_elements.personal_brand_integration: Shares behind-the-scenes operational realities and technical challenges
storytelling_elements.authority_building: References Fortune 500 work and technical complexity without naming clients
storytelling_elements.authenticity_signals: Admits when things are difficult or when he doesn't know something
storytelling_elements.relationship_focus: Prioritizes long-term strategic relationships over short-term transactions
